name: MANHATTAN BEER
tagline: From Montauk to Midtown to Monticello

lede.legalName: Manhattan Beer and Beverage Distributors, LLC (MBBD)
lede.industry: Beer, wine, spirits & non-alcoholic beverage distribution
lede.coverage: 14+ counties across New York City, Long Island and the Hudson Valley
lede.narrative: Founded in 1978 by Simon Bergson out of a 4,000-sq-ft Bronx warehouse with one truck and two brands, Manhattan Beer Distributors grew over nearly five decades into the largest single-market beer distributor in the United States and the fourth-largest beverage distributor in the country. Breakthroughs came in 1988, when Bergson won the New York state contract for Corona, and in 1998, when a merger with the city's Coors distributor nearly tripled volume overnight from roughly 6 million to 17 million cases. Today the company runs five facilities totaling over 1.2 million sq ft, serves ~25,000 retail customers across 14+ counties 'from Montauk to Midtown to Monticello,' and operates one of the largest privately owned alternative-fuel delivery fleets in the region. In January 2025 it rebranded to Manhattan Beer and Beverage Distributors to reflect a portfolio that now spans imports, craft, RTDs, energy, water, wine and spirits. Founder Simon Bergson — 'New York's beer king' — remains President and CEO, with son Alex Bergson rising as next-generation successor.

timeline.title: Founded in the Bronx
timeline.body: Simon Bergson launches Manhattan Beer following the closing of the Rheingold Brewery, starting with a 4,000-sq-ft warehouse, three delivery trucks and distribution rights to two brands — Carling Black Label and Tuborg.

timeline.title: Wins the Corona contract
timeline.body: Bergson secures the New York state distribution rights to Corona, a pivotal win that anchored the company's dominant import portfolio and fueled rapid growth.

timeline.title: Merges with NYC's Coors distributor
timeline.body: Manhattan Beer merges with the city's Coors distributor, nearly tripling annual volume overnight from roughly 6 million to 17 million cases and cementing Coors Light and Corona as portfolio pillars.

timeline.title: Crosses 28 million cases
timeline.body: The company is delivering 28+ million cases annually across the five NYC boroughs from a portfolio of about 40 brands, ranking as the largest single-market beer distributor in the U.S. with over $570M in sales.

timeline.title: Pioneers CNG heavy-duty fleet
timeline.body: Manhattan Beer becomes one of the first U.S. companies to deploy compressed-natural-gas heavy-duty delivery trucks, backed by ~$1.6M from NYSERDA plus $4.6M of its own funds, targeting Bronx-area air quality.

timeline.title: Opens Wyandanch / Long Island facility
timeline.body: The company expands onto Long Island with a distribution center in Wyandanch, extending reach toward Montauk and growing its footprint beyond New York City.

timeline.title: Agrees to absorb rival Windmill (Phoenix/Beehive)
timeline.body: Manhattan Beer reaches a tentative deal to merge with Brooklyn-based Windmill Distributing (Phoenix/Beehive), which sold ~13M cases/yr — a combination giving Manhattan roughly half of NYC's ~100M-case beer market.

timeline.title: Closes Windmill consolidation
timeline.body: The Windmill/Phoenix acquisition closes, consolidating the two largest beer wholesalers in New York City and adding craft and import brands including Brooklyn Brewery and Anchor.

timeline.title: Beverage Industry Wholesaler of the Year
timeline.body: Named 2016 Wholesaler of the Year, recognized for continual growth, a diversified ~350-brand portfolio and sustainability leadership — by then 1,850 employees, 400+ trucks, 45M+ cases and 25,000 customers. Simon Bergson is inducted into the Brooklyn Jewish Hall of Fame.

timeline.title: Scales solar across rooftops
timeline.body: The company builds out one of the NYC area's largest privately owned solar programs, installing rooftop arrays across Bronx, Queens and later Suffern facilities supplying more than 3 MW of clean power.

timeline.title: First East Coast Volvo VNRe electric trucks
timeline.body: Manhattan Beer becomes the first East Coast customer to purchase Volvo's VNRe heavy-duty battery-electric trucks, installing 14 EV chargers at its Bronx HQ alongside its CNG fleet and on-site fueling stations.

timeline.title: ~300 CNG trucks and rail-led logistics
timeline.body: Roughly 300 of ~400 trucks run on compressed natural gas via three private fueling stations; nearly half of all product arrives by rail, removing tens of thousands of tractor-trailer trips from local roads each year.

timeline.title: Opens $80M automated Suffern facility
timeline.body: In December 2024 the company unveils a modernized Suffern (Hudson Valley) distribution center built with Westfalia Technologies — a seven-level high-density AS/RS in a new 100,000-sq-ft, 55-ft-tall addition that lifts storage from ~850,000 to 1.5M+ cases, with FEFO expiration-date picking, a 1-MW rooftop solar array and a 100% renewable-natural-gas fleet.

timeline.title: Rebrands to Manhattan Beer & Beverage Distributors
timeline.body: On January 9, 2025, the company rebrands as Manhattan Beer and Beverage Distributors (MBBD) to reflect a diversified portfolio spanning beer, RTDs (White Claw, Truly, Surfside, Sun Cruiser), energy/hydration (Zoa, Mas+, Guayaki), water, wine and spirits.

timeline.title: Showcases fleet sustainability nationally
timeline.body: Senior director of fleet operations & sustainability Juan Corcino presents Manhattan Beer's CNG-plus-electric logistics transformation at the 2025 Advanced Clean Transportation (ACT) Expo in Anaheim.

news.headline: Manhattan Beer debuts $80M automated Suffern facility
news.summary: The distributor unveiled a modernized Hudson Valley DC built with Westfalia Technologies featuring a seven-level high-density AS/RS in a new 100,000-sq-ft addition, raising storage from ~850,000 to 1.5M+ cases with FEFO freshness picking, plus a 1-MW solar array and a 100% renewable-natural-gas fleet.

news.headline: Manhattan Beer Distributors unveils new name
news.summary: The 47-year-old company rebranded to Manhattan Beer and Beverage Distributors (MBBD) to reflect its expansion beyond beer into wine, spirits, RTDs, energy drinks, water and mixers across 14 counties.

news.headline: System report: high-density AS/RS automation at Suffern
news.summary: A detailed system report covers how the Westfalia-built automated storage and retrieval system boosted distribution-center efficiency, density and inventory freshness at Manhattan Beer's Suffern site.

news.headline: First East Coast buyer of Volvo VNRe electric trucks
news.summary: Manhattan Beer became the first East Coast customer to purchase Volvo's VNRe heavy-duty battery-electric trucks and installed 14 EV chargers at its Bronx headquarters, partnering with bp pulse on charging infrastructure.

news.headline: Manhattan Beer buys Suffern facility
news.summary: The company acquired and began redeveloping its Suffern, NY property in the Hudson Valley, the site that would become its flagship automated distribution center.

news.headline: Manhattan Beer presents fleet transformation at ACT Expo 2025
news.summary: Senior director of fleet operations and sustainability Juan Corcino joined a logistics panel at the Advanced Clean Transportation Expo, detailing the company's mix of ~300 CNG trucks, electric Volvo VNRe units and on-site fueling.

news.headline: Manhattan Beer Distributors gulps down rival
news.summary: Crain's reported Manhattan Beer's tentative deal to merge with Brooklyn-based Windmill Distributing, a combination that would give the company roughly half of NYC's beer market.

suppliers: Constellation Brands (Corona, Modelo Especial, Pacifico, Victoria)
Molson Coors (Coors Light, Coors Banquet, Miller Lite, Blue Moon)
The Boston Beer Company (Samuel Adams, Truly, Twisted Tea, Sun Cruiser)
Nestlé Waters (San Pellegrino, Acqua Panna, Perrier)
highlights: Imports lead the book (~50%): Corona, Modelo Especial, Pacifico, Heineken, Dos Equis, Tecate, Guinness
Domestic core (~40%): Coors Light, Coors Banquet, Miller Lite, Blue Moon, Genesee
Craft (~10%): Brooklyn Brewery, New Belgium, Captain Lawrence, Boston Beer
RTDs / FABs: White Claw, Truly, Twisted Tea, Surfside, Sun Cruiser
Energy & hydration: Red Bull, Zoa Energy, Mas+, Guayaki Yerba Mate
Premium water: Voss, San Pellegrino, Acqua Panna, Perrier

facilities.name: Bronx headquarters
facilities.note: Corporate HQ and flagship DC; 14 EV chargers, rooftop solar, LED-converted; anchors the largest single-market beer operation in the U.S.

facilities.name: Brooklyn facility
facilities.note: Serves Brooklyn and surrounding boroughs; absorbed volume from the 2015 Windmill/Phoenix consolidation.

facilities.name: Queens facility
facilities.note: Borough distribution center with rooftop solar contributing to the 3+ MW private solar program.

facilities.name: Wyandanch / Long Island facility
facilities.note: Long Island distribution hub extending coverage east toward Montauk.

facilities.name: Suffern automated DC
facilities.note: $80M Westfalia-built site (opened Dec 2024): seven-level AS/RS, 100,000-sq-ft / 55-ft addition, 1.5M+ case capacity, FEFO picking, 1-MW solar, 100% renewable-natural-gas fleet.

pullQuotes.text: Today, we have 1,850 employees and operate more than 400 trucks, providing service to 25,000 customers.
pullQuotes.cite: Bill Bessette, Chief Strategy Officer — Beverage Industry, 2016

pullQuotes.text: We are moving toward 100 percent use of CNG. The Bronx, where our headquarters is located, has one of the highest rates of asthma in the country, and the move to CNG helped reduce air pollution.

people.name: Simon Bergson
people.meta: 'New York's beer king'; founded the company in 1978, won Corona (1988) and merged with the Coors distributor (1998); Brooklyn Jewish Hall of Fame inductee (2016).

people.name: Mitchel Bergson
people.meta: Son of Simon Bergson; principal of MBBD overseeing generational transition.

people.name: Alex Bergson
people.meta: Son of Simon Bergson and designated next-generation successor; previously Director of Development & Innovation.

executives.name: Bill Bessette
executives.meta: Strategy lead; former chairman, New York State Beer Wholesalers' Association.

executives.name: Edward (Ed) McBrien
executives.meta: Operations chief; formerly president of sales & distributor operations at MillerCoors.

executives.name: George Wertheimer
executives.meta: Oversees finance for the ~$640M+ revenue operation.

executives.name: Richard Kleberg
executives.meta: Leads HR and people functions across ~1,850 employees.

executives.name: Juan Corcino
executives.meta: Leads CNG/electric fleet transition; ACT Expo 2025 panelist.

flags.description: The 2014-2015 absorption of rival Windmill/Phoenix gave Manhattan Beer roughly half of NYC's ~100M-case beer market, drawing scrutiny over pricing power; deal required supplier and DOJ review.
Manhattan Beer was accused in litigation of 'nickel and diming' customers for millions via disputed fees/charges.
Third-party aggregators report widely varying revenue ($570M-$750M) and employee figures (804-1,850); company/primary-press figures (~1,850 employees, ~$640M revenue) are more reliable.
